name: Minnesota Power Employees
description_short: Minnesota Power Employees Credit Union is a federally-insured credit union serving employees and their families with competitive CD rates, savings accounts, and comprehensive financial services from their Duluth, MN headquarters.
description_long:
Minnesota Power Employees Credit Union (MPECU) is a member-owned financial cooperative based in Duluth, Minnesota, operating at 30 W Superior St Ste 126. As a credit union, it serves Minnesota Power employees and their families with deposit and lending products. The institution has maintained operations as a trusted financial institution within the employee community it serves.

MPECU offers a range of deposit products including share certificates (CDs) across multiple term lengths from 3 months to 60 months, IRA certificates, money market accounts, and savings accounts. Their CD rates as of March 24, 2026 range from 2.45% APY (36-month) to 2.99% APY (6-month), with a consistent $500 minimum deposit requirement across all certificate products. The credit union also provides standard checking and savings services typical of full-service financial institutions.

The institution distinguishes itself through exceptional financial health metrics. MonitorBankRates assigns MPECU a 5.0 star rating and A+ overall health grade based on a Texas Ratio of 2.03%—significantly lower than the 6.13% national average. This metric indicates MPECU maintains very low non-performing assets relative to its capital reserves, signifying a strong and stable balance sheet. The independent verification of rates and third-party health assessments provide transparency for prospective members.

MPECU is best suited for Minnesota Power employees and their families seeking straightforward deposit products with competitive rates and institutional stability. The credit union structure provides member ownership benefits, though prospective customers should verify membership eligibility requirements, which typically restrict access to employees of specific employers or their families. The product offerings are traditional rather than innovative, focusing on reliability over digital-first banking experiences. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: IRA certificates across multiple term lengths
Checking accounts (implied through full-service credit union offerings)
pros: Exceptional financial health with A+ safety grade and 5.0 star rating from MonitorBankRates
Texas Ratio of 2.03%, significantly lower than 6.13% national average, indicating strong capital position
Competitive CD rates ranging from 2.45% to 2.99% APY across various terms (March 2026)
Low $500 minimum deposit requirement for all certificate products
Multiple CD term options from 3 months to 60 months to match savings timelines
IRA certificate products available alongside standard share certificates
Federally insured credit union providing NCUA protection for deposits
Verified rates sourced directly from official institution website with transparent rate tracking
cons: Membership likely restricted to Minnesota Power employees and immediate family—eligibility verification required before account opening
Savings account rates at 0.00% APY offer no yield on liquid savings balances
No money market account rates displayed on the reviewed profile despite mention of money market products
Limited online presence and digital banking information compared to larger national credit unions
CD rates show declining trend across longer terms, with 24-month and 36-month products below 2.5% APY
best_for: Minnesota Power employees seeking employer-affiliated financial services with verified stability
Savers with fixed timelines who want to lock in competitive CD rates with low minimum deposits
Members prioritizing institutional financial health and safety over digital banking features
Families of Minnesota Power employees eligible for credit union membership
diagnosis: Minnesota Power Employees Credit Union is best for Minnesota Power employees and their eligible families seeking stable, federally-insured savings products with competitive CD rates backed by exceptional financial health metrics. The main caveat is membership eligibility—this is an employer-affiliated credit union, so access is restricted to specific employee groups and their immediate families, making it unsuitable for the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
